note_data.title: Further kinetics
keyPoints: Reaction kinetics studies the rates at which chemical reactions occur
The effect of concentration on rate of reaction is a key factor to understand
Temperature has a significant effect on the rate of reaction
Catalysis plays an important role in affecting reaction rates
Enzymes are biological catalysts that affect reaction rates
Rate of reaction is covered as part of the broader chemistry curriculum alongside equilibrium

definitions.definition: The study of rates of reaction
Process involving catalysts that affect reaction rates

howToAnswer.instruction: Give the rate equation or order directly without explanation
Give the observation plus the reason using kinetics principles
Use given data to work out order, then write rate equation
Propose a mechanism consistent with the given rate equation

modelAnswer.question: The reaction between compounds P and Q follows the rate equation: rate = k[P]ВІ[Q]. Explain what happens to the rate when the concentration of P is doubled and the concentration of Q is halved.
modelAnswer.answer: When [P] is doubled, the rate increases by a factor of 2ВІ = 4 because P is second order. When [Q] is halved, the rate decreases by a factor of 2 because Q is first order. Overall effect = 4 Г— 0.5 = 2, so the rate doubles.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Rate increases by factor of 4 when [P] doubled
Mark 2: Rate decreases by factor of 2 when [Q] halved

commonMistakes: Not understanding the relationship between concentration and rate
Confusing the effects of temperature on reaction rate
Misunderstanding how catalysts work in reactions
examinerTips: Focus on understanding the effect of concentration on rate of reaction
Make sure to distinguish between different types of catalysts including enzymes
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Concentration-time graphs showing different orders: zero order (straight line), first order (exponential decay), second order (steeper curve). Also energy profile diagrams showing how catalysts lower activation energy without affecting overall enthalpy change.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) вЂ” they asked students to explain how temperature and concentration affect reaction rate using collision theory and calculate rate constants
N/D 2022 P2 Q5 (8 marks) вЂ” students had to interpret a rate-concentration graph, determine reaction order, and write a rate equation for a multi-step mechanism
M/J 2022 P1 Q15 (1 mark) вЂ” multiple choice question on identifying the correct units for rate constant when given a rate equation
N/D 2023 P2 Q3 (5 marks) вЂ” candidates analyzed experimental data to determine reaction order and calculate half-life for a first-order reaction

workedExample.question: The reaction A + B в†’ C was studied at different concentrations. The following data was obtained: Experiment 1: [A] = 0.10 mol dmвЃ»Ві, [B] = 0.10 mol dmвЃ»Ві, rate = 2.0 Г— 10вЃ»вЃґ mol dmвЃ»Ві sвЃ»В№. Experiment 2: [A] = 0.20 mol dmвЃ»Ві, [B] = 0.10 mol dmвЃ»Ві, rate = 8.0 Г— 10вЃ»вЃґ mol dmвЃ»Ві sвЃ»В№. Experiment 3: [A] = 0.10 mol dmвЃ»Ві, [B] = 0.20 mol dmвЃ»Ві, rate = 4.0 Г— 10вЃ»вЃґ mol dmвЃ»Ві sвЃ»В№. Determine the rate equation and calculate the rate constant. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Compare experiments 1 and 2 to find order with respect to A. [A] doubles, rate increases by factor of 4 (8.0/2.0), so rate в€ќ [A]ВІ, order = 2
Step 2: Compare experiments 1 and 3 to find order with respect to B. [B] doubles, rate doubles (4.0/2.0), so rate в€ќ [B]В№, order = 1
Step 3: Write rate equation: rate = k[A]ВІ[B]
Step 4: Calculate rate constant using experiment 1 data: 2.0 Г— 10вЃ»вЃґ = k Г— (0.10)ВІ Г— (0.10)
Step 5: Solve for k: k = (2.0 Г— 10вЃ»вЃґ)/(0.01 Г— 0.10) = 0.20 molвЃ»ВІ dmвЃ¶ sвЃ»В№
workedExample.answer: Rate equation: rate = k[A]ВІ[B]. Rate constant k = 0.20 molвЃ»ВІ dmвЃ¶ sвЃ»В№
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct order of A, 1 mark for correct order of B, 1 mark for correct rate equation, 2 marks for correct calculation setup, 1 mark for correct units

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused reaction order with stoichiometric coefficients' вЂ” always determine order experimentally from rate data, never assume it equals the balanced equation coefficients

Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently gave incorrect units for rate constants' вЂ” remember units depend on overall order: zero order = mol dmвЃ»Ві sвЃ»В№, first order = sвЃ»В№, second order = molвЃ»В№ dmВі sвЃ»В№

Examiner Report 2023: 'Poor explanations of how catalysts work at molecular level' вЂ” always mention that catalysts provide alternative reaction pathways with lower activation energy, not just 'speed up reactions'
note_data.keyDiagram: Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution curves showing effect of temperature on reaction rates. Draw two curves on same axes - lower temperature (higher peak, shifts left) and higher temperature (lower peak, shifts right). Mark activation energy Ea as vertical line. Shade areas under curves beyond Ea to show more molecules have sufficient energy at higher temperature. Label axes: y-axis 'Number of molecules', x-axis 'Kinetic energy'. Include arrow showing temperature increase.
note_data.quickRecap: Reaction kinetics is the study of rates of reaction. Key factors affecting reaction rates include concentration of reactants and temperature. Catalysis, including biological enzymes, plays an important role in controlling reaction rates.
